title: Remove dummy dizzy/cli.py
Add minimal worked example under examples/ (guestbook)
Rewrite README to show dizzy CLI workflow + example
Add agent-facing process doc (dizzy prime analog)
Generated Python code references command/event types in snake_case but LinkML emits CamelCase, so generated code cannot import its own models
Audit rust-cargo and typescript-npm lib generators for the same snake_case-vs-LinkML naming mismatch
Investigate configuring LinkML upstream to preserve snake_case class names (avoids CamelCase translation in dizzy generators)
Migrate to lib/-only architecture: remove src/ in favor of per-runtime lib/ packages
description: src/ (flat python dev tree from `dizzy gen`) is to be removed in favor of lib/ (per-runtime, per-element packages from `dizzy lib`), because lib/ is the path to multi-language support. Today the two trees are generated independently and don't share code: dizzy lib re-emits its own stubs (some degenerate, e.g. query stub is `def list_signatures() -> None`) and lib packages import gen_def/gen_int without declaring them as deps, so they aren't self-contained. Scope: make lib/ packages carry implementations, fix the degenerate lib stubs to match real protocol signatures, make each package self-contained (vendor or depend on generated types), update dizzy gen/lib + the guestbook example + demo.py + docs to drop src/.

title: rust-cargo/typescript-npm: generate protocols + contexts and real-signature lib stubs (parity with python-uv)
description: Follow-up from dizzy-e4a2 (lib/-only migration). python-uv element packages now carry real-signature implementation stubs (derived from gen_int Protocols/contexts) and depend on the gen_def/gen_int packages. The rust-cargo and typescript-npm lib generators still emit degenerate stubs (rust: 'pub fn <name>() { todo!() }'; ts equivalent) because those runtimes generate only gen_def types under lib/<runtime>/gen_def — they have no protocol/context generation. Scope: generate gen_int-equivalent protocol/context types for rust/ts, then emit element stubs with real signatures (command/event/input/context/output) matching the protocol, and make each element package depend on the runtime's generated types. Until then, rust/ts remain experimental and their stubs are signature-less.

title: Docs overhaul: docs/cli.md manpage, dizzy docs pages, generate verb rename
dizzy onboard: agent-facing overview verb
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy onboard (printed by 'dizzy docs'). Opinionated workflow layer over the docs pages: eight components, feature-file role, change taxonomy, exemplar good/bad events, command-per-lifecycle-step.

title: dizzy lint: deterministic feature-file checks
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy lint. Naming tense rules, fact-vs-derivation, PII-in-events, grain consistency, orphans, compat-mode checks. Nonzero exit on errors; JSON + pretty findings.

title: dizzy diff: structural feature-file diff
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy diff. Components/edges/slots added/removed/renamed/retyped; machine-readable change set classified additive / contract-changing / breaking / lossy-to-truth. Foundation for impact.

title: Spec gap: field-level projection slot mappings in feature-file/LinkML
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy impact, tier 1. Feature-file/LinkML must declare projection mappings at field granularity (which event slots map to which model columns) so slot-lineage walks are sound. Versioned feat-file format change — treat as compatibility-guaranteed spec.

title: dizzy impact: change impact subgraph with verdicts
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy impact. Four tiers: slot lineage, compiler oracle (worktree+typecheck), event-store compat, LLM semantic review. Verdicts must-change/must-review/unaffected-proven + recoverability bit. --emit seeds writes worklist in topo order (emitter behind small interface). Graph rendering is external repo; product here is the machine-readable verdict set.

title: dizzy simulate: LLM-driven feature-file execution
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy simulate. Phased event/command queue scheduler; narrow per-activation LLM calls with wiring-derived tools only; schema-validated payloads; report_finding tool (findings are the product); step budget + livelock detector; --level 0/1/2; append-only branchable session file; --provider/--exec/--manual modes.

title: dizzy scaffold: design-embedded implementation stubs
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy scaffold. Basic stubs ship in 'generate libraries' today; end state embeds feature-file description + mermaid as the docstring of every procedure/policy/projection/querier stub.

title: dizzy verify: conformance tests from contracts + golden traces
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy verify. Generate conformance tests from contracts; promote simulate session traces to golden tests run against real implementations.

title: dizzy compat: event schema evolution checks + upcaster scaffolds
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy compat. Old vs new schema → backward/forward/full verdict; scaffold upcasters for breaking changes; suggest optional-then-drop migrations for stored events.

title: dizzy rebuild <model>: replay event stream into fresh model
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy rebuild. Replay events through a projection into a fresh Model and cut over. The payoff move — polish far beyond its difficulty; converts migrations into rebuilds.

title: dizzy wrap: anti-corruption adapter for brownfield systems
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy wrap. Present an existing REST API / legacy DB as commands/queries/events (write-side adoption story; dj scripts are the read side).

title: dizzy deploy: hand-written deployment profiles
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy deploy. v1 = three hand-written profiles (monolith, per-component, one hybrid) selected by flag. No optimizer until real deployments produce real cost data.

title: simulate session file: versioned append-only JSONL run log (itself a DIZZY event stream) + deterministic replay
description: Plan pl-f395 step 5. Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy simulate (session file paragraph). Versioned append-only JSONL whose entries carry id/parentId so the file is a tree (prior art: pi agent session format — branching is in-place appending to an earlier parent, context rebuild walks leaf→root). Entry-type vocabulary seeded by the sim/ roleplay (PLAYBOOK.md Q5). Scheduler should emit the structured trace from day one; this step persists + versions + replays it.

title: simulate branching: replay to step N, swap modified feature-file, resume (counterfactual design probes)
description: Plan pl-f395 step 6. Deferred by design decision (2026-06-11): no branching semantics yet. With the pi-style id/parentId tree format from dizzy-e2d4, branching is largely a format property; the open question is divergence semantics when the swapped feature-file invalidates replayed activations (validate each logged step against new wiring; stop at first divergence). Define semantics before implementing.

title: dizzy validate: structural feature-file (+ scenario) validation verb
description: Requirements: docs/cli.md § dizzy validate. Expose load_feat+validate_feat as a CLI verb: parse, schema-check, referential integrity (commands/events/queries/models wiring), scenario-file cross-check. Nonzero exit with actionable errors. lint builds on top; all commands validate implicitly on load.

title: sim/: roleplay the simulate path by hand (PLAYBOOK.md) — answer Q1-Q5 before building
description:
Manual dry-run of dizzy simulate per sim/PLAYBOOK.md against sim/library.feat.yaml + scenarios. Products: design findings on the feat-file, and answers to Q1-Q5. Learnings update pl-f395 child seeds before the scheduler core (dizzy-3c9e) starts.

STATUS 2026-06-12 — RUN 3 IN FLIGHT, paused at finding t37 (the planted policy trap), awaiting Director ruling.
Run 3 (contested_reservation, session sim/sessions/contested_reservation__3.jsonl, t1-t37): finding t12 (member-code sequence scope, surfaced when a second minting server minted) resolved PER-SERVER as r4 — pending feat amendment at run end (enroll_member description must state per-server scope). Replay then ran clean through steps 3-6 (add_book, Ada borrows, Grace reserves, Ada returns). Step 7: policy fulfill_reservation_on_return activated on book_returned with NO query tools (wiring declares none) — component reported finding t37 missing-query-wiring rather than speculative-dispatch (rule 7) or silent healing (rule 3). The deliberate trap was caught by the component, not the harness.
Complete: run 1 (2 findings s7/s26, 7 rulings), branch 1b (closed at b8, insufficient-context), branch 1c (clean — verifies r1/r2/r3).
Answered: Q1 (querier sub-activation), Q2 (jmQ collapse), Q4 (scenario context section; timing open). Evolving: Q5. Q3 strengthening: v2 descriptions executed clean except where deliberately ambiguous/trapped.
Spawned: dizzy-c5a0 (command/event semantic enforcement knowledge seed).
Remaining for close: resolve t37, finish run 3 to quiescence, apply run-end feat amendments (r4 scope + t37 resolution), then fold learnings into pl-f395 children (esp. dizzy-e2d4 session format, dizzy-01cc level-2 meaning post-jmQ, dizzy-f87c manual-mode protocol).

title: Knowledge: stricter command/event semantic enforcement — commands must be impossible to construct without their required referents
description: Origin: sim run 1 (borrow_available_book__1.jsonl, findings s7+s26). borrow_book was injectable with no member identity; lend_book's deduction was sound but the command was never legitimate — the root cause sits at command construction, not at the handler. Directions to build on when this recurs: (1) level-1 slot schemas make required ids structural; (2) referential validity against the event store (does a member_registered fact exist for this id?) is stricter than schema validity — candidate enforcement points: scenario validation in dizzy validate, a simulate harness check at injection time, a lint rule, and wrap (the brownfield command factory). Insufficient information to change the playbook yet — accumulate instances here first.
